text:
His name is Caleb Oppong. Master artisan. He taught me everything I know about making things.

And here is what I noticed about him. He never rushed to the work. He would stand and look at the problem for a long time. Turning it over in his mind. Running through it. And then when he sat down, he moved with conviction. Every cut, every join, every decision had already been made before his hands touched anything.

That is what mastery looks like from the outside. Not speed. Clarity.

When you have seen something enough times, in your hands and in your mind, the decision is already made before the moment arrives. You are not figuring it out. You are executing what you already know.

I watched him and I understood something. Mastery is not talent. It is accumulated reps.
